title: Data Processing Principles
intent: Personal data must be processed lawfully, fairly, and transparently. Collection must be limited to necessary purposes (data minimization), only for explicit legitimate purposes (purpose limitation), accurate, stored only as long as needed, and processed securely.

requirements.text: Processed lawfully, fairly and in a transparent manner
checklist: Are purposes explained in plain language?
Is lawful basis linked to each purpose?
Is transparency demonstrated (no hidden use)?

requirements.text: Collected for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes
checklist: Are purposes listed clearly?
Is vague or generic language avoided?
Does the policy say only necessary data is collected?
Does the policy explain how data is kept accurate?
Is there a way for users to correct data?
Are retention periods or criteria stated?
Is there a statement on deleting data when no longer needed?
Are security measures mentioned?
Is confidentiality emphasized?

examples: we only collect the minimum information necessary to provide the service
data is used solely to improve our product
we retain account data for 12 months after inactivity
we take reasonable steps to ensure data accuracy
access controls and logging are used to protect user data

title: Lawfulness of Processing
intent: Processing is lawful only if it meets one of six conditions: consent, contract, legal obligation, vital interests, public task, or legitimate interests.

checklist: Is consent mentioned as freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous?
Is withdrawal of consent possible and explained?
Is processing necessary for a contract or pre-contract steps?
Is this clearly explained in the policy?
Is processing based on a legal requirement mentioned?
Is protection of life or vital interests cited as a basis?
Is processing for official authority or public interest noted?
Is legitimate interest identified?
Is balancing against user rights mentioned?

examples: we process your data with your consent
processing is necessary to fulfill our contract
we retain data to comply with legal obligations
we rely on legitimate interests to prevent fraud

title: Transparent Information, Communication and Modalities
intent: Controllers must provide clear, concise, and accessible information to data subjects about how their data is processed and how to exercise rights.

requirements.text: Information in a concise, transparent, intelligible and easily accessible form
checklist: Is the policy written in clear and plain language?
Is information structured and easy to find?
Does the policy explain how rights can be exercised?
Are contact channels provided for requests?
Does the policy mention responses within 1 month?
Is there reference to possible extension for complex requests?

examples: we provide this policy in plain language
you can contact our privacy team to exercise your rights
we will respond to your request within one month

title: Information to be Provided (Data Collected from Data Subject)
intent: When data is collected directly, the controller must provide identity, contact details, processing purposes, lawful basis, recipients, retention, rights, and transfer details.

requirements.text: Identity and contact details of the controller
checklist: Is the company identified?
Are contact details given?
Is a DPO identified?
Are DPO contact details provided?
Are purposes explicitly listed?
Is a lawful basis linked to each purpose?
Are recipients or categories listed (processors, third parties)?
Is data transfer outside the EU mentioned?
Are safeguards described (SCCs, adequacy, etc.)?
Is retention period/criteria provided?
Are data subject rights listed?
Is right to complain to authority included?

examples: our company is the data controller
you may contact our Data Protection Officer
we process your data to deliver our services
we transfer data outside the EU with SCCs

title: Information to be Provided (Data Not Collected from Data Subject)
intent: When data is obtained indirectly, the controller must inform about categories of data, source, and provide the same information required under Article 13.

checklist: Are categories of collected data specified?
Is the source mentioned?
Is it stated if the source is publicly available?

triggers: we collect information from third parties
examples: we may receive your data from third-party partners
we collect information from publicly available databases

title: Right of Access
intent: Data subjects have the right to access their personal data and obtain confirmation, a copy, and related information.

checklist: Is the right of access mentioned?
Is there a way to request data copies?
Does the policy say data subjects can obtain a copy of their personal data?

examples: you have the right to access your personal data
you may request a copy of the information we hold

title: General Principle for Transfers
intent: Transfers of personal data outside the EU/EEA must only occur if safeguards are in place.

checklist: Is data transfer outside the EU acknowledged?
Are safeguards explained?

triggers: data may be stored in other countries
examples: we may transfer your data outside the European Economic Area
data is transferred internationally with appropriate safeguards

title: Transfers on the Basis of Adequacy
intent: Transfers are allowed if the Commission has decided a third country ensures adequate protection.

checklist: Does the policy state transfers are based on adequacy decisions?

examples: we transfer data to countries recognized by the European Commission as providing adequate protection

title: Transfers Subject to Appropriate Safeguards
intent: Where no adequacy decision exists, transfers must rely on safeguards such as SCCs, BCRs, codes of conduct, or certification.

checklist: Does the policy mention SCCs, BCRs, or other safeguards?

examples: we use Standard Contractual Clauses approved by the European Commission
data is transferred under binding corporate rules

title: Binding Corporate Rules
intent: Intra-group transfers may be based on binding corporate rules approved by authorities.

checklist: Does the policy mention binding corporate rules for intra-group transfers?

examples: our group companies rely on approved binding corporate rules

title: Transfers or Disclosures Not Authorised by Union Law
intent: Requests for personal data by third country authorities are only recognized if based on international agreements.

checklist: Does the policy mention government requests from outside EU?
Does it say such requests require international agreement?

examples: we only disclose data to foreign authorities if based on an international agreement

title: Derogations for Specific Situations
intent: Transfers may occur exceptionally with explicit consent, contract performance, public interest, legal claims, vital interests, or public registers.

checklist: Does the policy mention transfers based on explicit consent?
Does the policy mention transfers necessary for a contract?
Is transfer for important reasons of public interest stated?

examples: we may transfer your data outside the EU with your explicit consent
cross-border transfer may be required to perform a contract with you
